message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="orange" summary="+1 on atomicity concern, frontend-specific detail">
+1 on your analysis, Kent. I raised the same atomicity concern in my earlier message.

To add specificity from the frontend side: the CSS alone (168 new lines in main.css) plus three new templates plus the inline JS for empty-state toggling could easily have been their own atomic step, committed after the domain/infrastructure layers were already green and reviewed. That would have given the design-focused reviewers (Steve Schoger, Heydon, me) a much tighter scope to review meaningfully, rather than evaluating CSS alongside 500+ lines of Rust service code.

I agree with the proposed improvement: decompose into smaller commits per layer or slice. For Step 4 (Next Actions), I would expect to see the CSS/template work as its own commit, reviewed separately from the domain modeling and route handlers.

No other process concerns from me beyond what I already noted (the inline JS vs HX-Trigger pattern for empty-state toggling -- worth standardizing but not blocking).
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good reinforcement from Lea — making the practical point that smaller commits would let domain-focused and frontend-focused reviewers each review tighter, more relevant diffs. Seven in. Waiting on Heydon and Marty.
